brief_title: Empagliflozin for Kidney Outcomes in Patients with Chronic Kidney Disease and Type 2 Diabetes
official_title: A Phase 3, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study of Empagliflozin 10mg in Patients with Chronic Kidney Disease and Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (EMPA-KIDNEY-DM)
brief_summary: This Phase 3 study evaluates the renal protective effects of empagliflozin in patients with chronic kidney disease and type 2 diabetes. The primary composite endpoint is sustained decline in eGFR of >= 40%, end-stage kidney disease, renal death, or cardiovascular death.
raw_inclusion_text:
1. Adults aged 18-85 years

2. Diagnosis of type 2 diabetes mellitus

3. Chronic kidney disease with eGFR 25-60 mL/min/1.73m² at screening

4. Urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) >= 200 mg/g at screening

5. On stable dose of ACE inhibitor or ARB at maximum tolerated dose for >= 4 weeks

6. HbA1c 6.5-12.0%

7. Systolic blood pressure <= 160 mmHg and diastolic <= 100 mmHg

8. On stable antidiabetic therapy for >= 8 weeks

9. BMI 20-45 kg/m²

10. Written informed consent
raw_exclusion_text:
1. Type 1 diabetes mellitus

2. Non-diabetic kidney disease (polycystic kidney disease, lupus nephritis, etc.)

3. eGFR < 25 mL/min/1.73m² or on dialysis

4. Kidney transplant recipient

5. History of diabetic ketoacidosis within past 6 months

6. NYHA Class III-IV heart failure

7. Active urinary tract infection at screening

8. History of recurrent genital infections (>= 4 episodes/year)

9. Pregnant or breastfeeding

10. Use of SGLT2 inhibitor within past 8 weeks

inclusion_criteria.text: Chronic kidney disease with eGFR 25-60 mL/min/1.73m² at screening
Urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) >= 200 mg/g at screening
On stable dose of ACE inhibitor or ARB at maximum tolerated dose for >= 4 weeks
Systolic blood pressure <= 160 mmHg and diastolic <= 100 mmHg
On stable antidiabetic therapy for >= 8 weeks

exclusion_criteria.text: Non-diabetic kidney disease (polycystic kidney disease, lupus nephritis, etc.)
History of diabetic ketoacidosis within past 6 months
Active urinary tract infection at screening
History of recurrent genital infections (>= 4 episodes/year)
Use of SGLT2 inhibitor within past 8 weeks
